title: advance_diversifier_to silently ignores corrupt diversifier_index
description: advance_diversifier_to reads diversifier_index with .and_then(|s: String| s.parse().ok()), turning a parse failure into None. A None current bypasses the monotonicity guard and overwrites with idx, even if the corrupt string encoded a value larger than idx. This violates the monotonicity invariant the function exists to protect, without any error signal to the caller. next_diversifier and get_diversifier_index both propagate parse errors with ?; the inconsistency means a corrupt DB causes silent address reuse in advance_diversifier_to but a hard failure in next_diversifier — the worst combination. Fix: propagate the parse error (use the same ? pattern as next_diversifier).
